runtime_boundary: Option-B/C hybrid: runtime-owned realized fp16 cache buffer; __call__ BYPASSES @function(precompile=True) for the decode route (so the opaque append persists in the realized buffer, not via @function); _attention does opaque append + owned-tile read of the persistent cache.

progress.microbench: RUNTIME_KV_MICROBENCH_PASS -- isolated runtime cache + opaque append + owned tile, TinyJit replay: persistence accumulates across steps (rel_rmse ~3.8e-7), reset/no-stale, no full-MAXC copy, append-before-tile.
progress.function_bypass_safe: decode is byte-identical WITHOUT @function (monkeypatch test) -> bypassing @function for the runtime route is correctness-safe.
progress.fp16_dtype_fix: the canonical cache is dtypes.float (fp32) but the opaque append kernel writes fp16 (b16 stores) -> fp32 cache = wrong byte offsets = no persistence. Forcing the runtime cache to fp16 (matching the append kernel + owned tile + microbench) FIXED eager persistence.
progress.eager_persists: with fp16 cache, EAGER multi-step appends persist (positions 2048,2049,2050 written); the runtime route fires 36x/forward (one per layer).
progress.prefill_handoff: prefill (canonical store, ctx<2048, @function kept) persists into the fp16 cache (positions written); decode reads it (first decode token byte-identical to baseline).

blocker.jit_replay_position_baking: under TinyJit decode replay, the opaque append's WRITE POSITION bakes at the CAPTURE start_pos: after 4 decode steps (sp 2050,2051,2052,2053) only the captured position 2050 is written; replays do NOT advance the write -> positions 2051+ stay zero -> garbage after the first token.
blocker.contrast: the microbench's append ADVANCES correctly across TinyJit replays (its K/V src is an external tensor, independent of start_pos). The model's append K/V depend on start_pos via rope (freqs_cis[start_pos:start_pos+T]), which appears to concretize start_pos for the append launch -> the write position bakes. (baseline canonical store advances correctly, so start_pos itself replays; the opaque-append-with-start_pos-dependent-src is the differentiator.)
blocker.owned_tile_ctx_restriction: the owned tile (the opaque read) is correct only at ctx>=2048 (short-ctx over-split NaN), so even without the baking the route could not clear +5%@ctx1024.

graph_identity_when_fired.note: when the runtime route fires (decode capture-ctx>=2048): NO full-MAXC copy, owned tile reads the persistent cache, no gqa fallback. Copy removal confirmed.
